name: Cuisinart ICE-21 1.5-Qt Automatic Ice Cream Maker

specs.Outputs: Ice cream, sorbet, gelato, frozen yogurt, sherbet

whatWorks: Churns a liquid base into fully frozen ice cream, sorbet, or gelato in 20–25 minutes — faster than most budget machines and with consistently smooth results when the bowl is properly pre-frozen.
The double-walled insulated freezer bowl eliminates the need for rock salt or ice, making the process genuinely simple — freeze the bowl, pour in the base, turn on the machine.
Quiet enough to run on the counter while cooking, and compact enough to store the bowl in a standard freezer without displacing other items.
At $79 CAD, it's the most accessible entry point into home ice cream making in Canada, and owners consistently report results that rival commercial gelato at a fraction of the per-serving cost.
worthKnowing: The freezer bowl must pre-freeze for 6–24 hours before use — spontaneous ice cream is not possible, and planning ahead the night before is essentially required.
The machine produces soft-serve consistency at the end of the churn cycle — for scoopable ice cream, transfer to a container and freeze for an additional 2 hours.
1.5 Qt is a small batch — roughly 3–4 adult servings — which means multiple churn cycles (and multiple bowl freeze sessions) for larger gatherings.
The bowl takes up a meaningful amount of freezer space when stored, which can be a real constraint in a compact apartment freezer.

commonComplaints.title: Have to plan 24 hours ahead or…
commonComplaints.body: Have to plan 24 hours ahead. I've forgotten to freeze the bowl more times than I can count. It requires habit, not impulse.

commonComplaints.title: Ice cream needs 2 more hours in…
commonComplaints.body: Ice cream needs 2 more hours in the freezer after churning to get scoopable. The machine makes soft serve, not hard ice cream.

commonComplaints.title: 1.5 Qt is not enough for a…
commonComplaints.body: 1.5 Qt is not enough for a family of four. We ran it twice in a row for a birthday party and still didn't have enough.

commonComplaints.title: Bowl takes up a big chunk of…
commonComplaints.body: Bowl takes up a big chunk of my freezer space. Barely fits in my apartment freezer beside everything else.

verdict: The Cuisinart ICE-21 is the clearest entry point into home ice cream making in Canada — it's affordable, produces genuinely good results, and the process is simple once you build the habit of freezing the bowl in advance. The planning requirement and the need for a second 2-hour freeze to achieve scoopable texture are real, not minor, constraints. For households that plan ahead and want good homemade ice cream without a large investment, it delivers exactly what it promises.

realBuyers.quote: Made pistachio gelato that was better than what I get at the gelato shop near my house. Couldn't believe it.
My kids picked their own mix-ins and were obsessed with the whole process. We make a batch every weekend now.
Really simple to use. Freeze bowl, make base, pour in, 25 minutes later — ice cream. Worth every dollar.
Keep the bowl in the freezer permanently and you're always 25 minutes away from ice cream. Game changer for the summer.

metaDescription: Cuisinart ICE-21 review for Canada: 1.5-Qt automatic ice cream maker that churns ice cream, sorbet, and gelato in 20–25 minutes. Honest on bowl pre-freeze and soft-serve output. ~$79 CAD.
introText: The Cuisinart ICE-21 is a 1.5-Qt automatic ice cream maker that churns homemade ice cream, sorbet, and gelato in 20–25 minutes using a pre-frozen double-walled bowl. It's the most accessible ice cream maker in Canada and available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B000FMQHM0) at around $79 CAD.
